zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke於1172年出生在英國威爾斯布洛可爾布洛可時，她的父親Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke42歲，母親Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster27歲。她於1189年8月8日與William Marshal Earl of Pembroke在英國米德萊可倫敦結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1220年在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾坡斯托福享年48歲，埋葬在威爾士蒙姆爾特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke于1172年出生在英国威尔斯布洛可爾布洛可时，她的父亲Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke42岁，母亲Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster27岁。她于1189年8月8日与William Marshal Earl of Pembroke在英国米德萊可倫敦结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1220年在英国威尔斯蒙姆爾坡斯托福享年48岁，埋葬在威尔士蒙姆爾特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke was born in 1172, in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke, was 42 and her mother, Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster, was 27. She married William Marshal Earl of Pembroke on 8 August 1189, in London, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 6 daughters. She died in 1220, in Chepstow, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 48, and was buried in Tintern, Monmouthshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke est née en 1172, à Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé William Marshal Earl of Pembroke le 8 août 1189, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée en 1220, à Chepstow, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Tintern, Monmouthshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke 1172 in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke 42 und ihre Mutter Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster 27. Sie hat am 8. August 1189 in London, Middlesex, England William Marshal Earl of Pembroke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb 1220 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Chepstow, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom und wurde in Tintern, Monmouthshire, Wales begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke è nata il nel 1172 nel Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Galles, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato William Marshal Earl of Pembroke il 8 agosto 1189, nel London, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel 1220, nel Chepstow, Monmouthshire, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolta a Tintern, Monmouthshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembrokeが1172年にイギリスウェールズペンブロケシュアイアペンブロクで生まれた時，父Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembrokeは42歳，母Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinsterは27歳であった。1189年8月8日にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンでWilliam Marshal Earl of Pembrokeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1220年にイギリスウェールズモンモウスシュアイアチェプストウで48歳で死去し，ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアティンテルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke(이)가 1172년 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르 p브로크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1189년 8월 8일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 William Marshal Earl of Pembroke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1220년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르 체프스타우에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 몬모우스실르 틴터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke nasceu em 1172, em Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com William Marshal Earl of Pembroke em 8 de agosto de 1189, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1220, em Chepstow, Monmouthshire, Gales, United Kingdom, com 48 anos, e foi sepultada em Tintern, Monmouthshire, Gales.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke родилась в 1172 г. в местности пемброк, пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Marshal Earl of Pembroke 8 августа 1189 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла в 1220 г. в местности чепстоу, монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности тинтирн, монмоутшир, Уэльс.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de Clare Countess of Pembroke nació en 1172, en Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Princess Aoife MacMurrough of Leinster, tenía 27 años. Se casó con William Marshal Earl of Pembroke el 8 de agosto de 1189, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió en 1220, en Chepstow, Monmouthshire, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultada en Tintern, Monmouthshire, Gales.
